title: QA Fibonacci hypergraph structural theorems on S_9 — sliding window, uniform degree, orbit-multiset collapse
_fixture_purpose: Certifies the state-residue incidence hypergraph H(m) where each QA state (b,e) contributes a length-4 Fibonacci window hyperedge (b,e,d,a) with d=b+e, a=b+2e. Three structural theorems: (1) T acts as 1-step Fibonacci window shift, (2) vertex degrees uniform at 4m, (3) orbit-multiset distribution matches (22,22,22,4,1) on S_9 T-orbits.
source_attribution: Leonardo of Pisa (Fibonacci), 'Liber Abaci' (1202) — Fibonacci recurrence F_{n+1}=F_n+F_{n-1}. Edouard Lucas, 'Theorie des Fonctions Numeriques Simplement Periodiques' (American Journal of Mathematics, 1878). D. D. Wall, 'Fibonacci Series Modulo m' (American Mathematical Monthly 67, 1960, pp. 525-532) — Pisano period function pi(m). Claude Berge, 'Hypergraphs: Combinatorics of Finite Sets' (North-Holland, 1989) — hypergraph theory foundations. QA formalization: Will Dale + Claude, 2026-04-11.
theorem_statement: Let H(m) be the bipartite incidence hypergraph with vertex set V={1,...,m} and hyperedge set E={(b,e,d,a) : (b,e) in S_m} where d=(b+e) mod m and a=(b+2e) mod m (A1-adjusted). Then: (1) the QA generator T=(b,e)->(e,d) acts on hyperedges as the 1-step Fibonacci window shift (F0,F1,F2,F3) -> (F1,F2,F3,F2+F3); (2) every vertex v in V has degree exactly 4m in H(m); (3) within each T-orbit of S_m, the set of distinct multiset hyperedges has size strictly less than or equal to the orbit length, with collapses determined by the internal symmetry of the Fibonacci-window sequence.
fibonacci_recurrence: F_{k+1} = F_{k-1} + F_k; in hyperedge terms, the slide shifts (F0,F1,F2,F3) -> (F1,F2,F3,F4) where F4 = F2 + F3 = d + a (mod m)

sliding_window_s9.formula: H(T(s)) = (e_s, d_s, a_s, (d_s + a_s) mod m)

vertex_degree_s9.formula: degree(v) = 4m; on S_9 this is 36 per vertex, total 4 * 81 = 324
vertex_degree_s9.proof_sketch: Each of the 4 hyperedge positions (b, e, d, a) takes value v for exactly m of the m^2 states in S_m (one equation per slot). Summing: 4m contributions per vertex, independent of v.

witnesses.note: Fibonacci window starts at (F_0,F_1,F_2,F_3) = (1,1,2,3); one slide gives (1,2,3,5), the next Fibonacci window. d+a = 2+3 = 5 = new last element. Canonical Fibonacci seed.
Satellite orbit slide: (3,3,6,9) -> (3,6,9,6) because d+a = 6+9 = 15 mod 9 = 6 (A1-adjusted). The 8-cycle produces only 4 distinct multisets due to internal period-2 symmetry.
Fixed point: (9,9,9,9). The singleton hyperedge {9} with multiplicity 4. Single multiset, single orbit.

verification_result: all_ok = true; sliding_window 81/81; vertex_degree uniform at 36; orbit_multisets match (22,22,22,4,1)
